The plan starts the day after the manufacturer’s warranty ends or on the 366th day from the date of device purchase, whichever is later. You can purchase this plan only for devices that have AT LEAST 1 Authorised Service Centre anywhere in India. The sum of the manufacturer’s warranty and extended warranty CANNOT EXCEED 5 YEARS. About this item

  • 𝐒𝐮𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐨𝐫 𝐒𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐝 𝐐𝐮𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲: Designed with a spruce top and rosewood fingerboard, the Yamaha F280 delivers rich, balanced tones perfect for all music styles.
  • 𝐁𝐞𝐠𝐢𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐫-𝐅𝐫𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐥𝐲 𝐃𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧: Full-size dreadnought body with a smooth neck profile ensures comfortable playability, making it ideal for beginners and intermediate players.
  • 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐮𝐦 𝐁𝐮𝐢𝐥𝐝 𝐌𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐚𝐥𝐬: Crafted with high-quality tonewoods and durable hardware for long-lasting performance and consistent tuning stability.
  • 𝐂𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐩 & 𝐂𝐥𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝐓𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐬: The natural finish enhances resonance, while precision construction ensures bright highs, clear mids, and deep lows.
  • 𝐋𝐨𝐰 𝐌𝐚𝐢𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐧𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞, 𝐇𝐢𝐠𝐡 𝐃𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐛𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐭𝐲: Built to withstand frequent use and varying climates, requiring minimal upkeep over time.

From the manufacturer

Yamaha music guitars

Yamaha music guitars are globally recognized for their exceptional craftsmanship, affordability, and musical versatility, making them a top choice for beginners, intermediate players, and even professional musicians. Whether acoustic or electric, Yamaha guitars consistently deliver quality sound, durability, and playability at every price point.

strings

Traditional Western body guitars (like dreadnoughts, jumbos, and slope-shoulder acoustics) are designed for steel strings, not nylon. These guitars rely on the tension and resonance of steel strings to deliver the loud, punchy, and full-bodied sound typical of Western music genres like country, folk, and bluegrass.

Durability

Here’s a detailed overview of the durability of Traditional Western body Yamaha guitars, with a focus on their construction, materials, build quality, and real-world performance. Yamaha has a strong reputation for making affordable, reliable acoustic guitars that hold up over time.

Neck Stability & Durability

Yamaha necks are less prone to warping because of:

Dense wood selection (Nato/Okoume)

Proper seasoning of the wood

Protective polyurethane finish

Reliable truss rod system.

Traditional Western Body Guitars

Traditional Western body guitars" typically refer to acoustic guitars designed with a large, rounded body shape commonly associated with cowboy or country music, popular in the American West. These guitars are known for their bold volume, deep bass response, and rich tone, making them ideal for strumming, flatpicking, and accompaniment in folk.

    How are ratings calculated?
    To calculate the overall star rating and percentage breakdown by star, we don’t use a simple average. Instead, our system considers things like how recent a review is and if the reviewer bought the item on Amazon. It also analyses reviews to verify trustworthiness.
    Sponsored

    Customers say

    Customers find this acoustic guitar to be a good entry-level instrument that sounds like a dream and is suitable for both beginners and experienced players. The guitar features a glossy finish on the top and matte finish on the sides and back, with impressive build quality. While customers consider it worth the price, some report receiving damaged instruments with dents and cracks.

    • Shariq
      5.0 out of 5 stars Full review
      Reviewed in India on 29 January 2026
      Colour: NATURALVerified Purchase
      My dream come true moment its a good guitar in budget i know its too much for many people and are searching for good reviews so here it is.

      From a beginner to a beginner i bought this 3 years ago just to set an impression in college but this becomes my love its a very good guitar with good strings and all .
      I have only changed the strings once coz its good to change time to time .
      Frets got a little rust not like a pure rust but soem blackness freets mattel parts which devide the frets only got the rust.

      Size is big if you dont have enough space or your hands are small you can have difficulty so you should also remember the size .

      In the end its a great guitar under 8k from agood brand yamaha beast brand in this category

    • Thang seldou
      4.0 out of 5 stars Affordable, average quality, Acoustic guitar for beginners.
      Reviewed in India on 22 December 2025
      Colour: NATURALVerified Purchase
      Yamaha has done a great job by manufacturing this decent, entry level dreadnought acoustic guitar aimed at the beginner level.

      I have been using the Yamaha F210 (very old model, purchased back in 2014) and did a quick comparison between the two. F280 produces a crispy and resonant sound almost similar to its higher priced brothers in the entry level segment, but lacks detailing in its design. The finishing is also not upto the mark and uses a black matte paint for binding the edges with slight imperfections(pictures shown) instead of layering.

      If you are on a tight budget, you may go for it, but if detailing and better tone is a concern, then the F310 will be a good choice.
      Overall, it is the best choice among acoustic guitars at this price.

      Also for buyers, I suggest you to buy a guitar case for storage and prevent contact with moisture as it can affect the sound quality in the long run.

      Edit after 1 month usage: I find Yamaha guitars manufactured now in India are not upto the mark regarding quality(My old F210 was manufactured in Indonesia). The black paint used to bind the edges have started fading off (pictures attached), which were not encountered in the F210 even after years of usage.
      Read more
      Thang seldou
      4.0 out of 5 stars
      Affordable, average quality, Acoustic guitar for beginners.

      Reviewed in India on 22 December 2025
      Yamaha has done a great job by manufacturing this decent, entry level dreadnought acoustic guitar aimed at the beginner level.

      I have been using the Yamaha F210 (very old model, purchased back in 2014) and did a quick comparison between the two. F280 produces a crispy and resonant sound almost similar to its higher priced brothers in the entry level segment, but lacks detailing in its design. The finishing is also not upto the mark and uses a black matte paint for binding the edges with slight imperfections(pictures shown) instead of layering.

      If you are on a tight budget, you may go for it, but if detailing and better tone is a concern, then the F310 will be a good choice.
      Overall, it is the best choice among acoustic guitars at this price.

      Also for buyers, I suggest you to buy a guitar case for storage and prevent contact with moisture as it can affect the sound quality in the long run.

      Edit after 1 month usage: I find Yamaha guitars manufactured now in India are not upto the mark regarding quality(My old F210 was manufactured in Indonesia). The black paint used to bind the edges have started fading off (pictures attached), which were not encountered in the F210 even after years of usage.

    • Waqas mahboob
      5.0 out of 5 stars About this product
      Reviewed in India on 10 January 2026
      Colour: NATURALVerified Purchase
      The product is quite good, but after placing the order four times, I finally received the correct product. Before this, I ordered three times and the delivery experience was very bad. Twice I received a defective product, and once the product was completely different—someone sent a Milton water tank instead of a guitar.

      Then I placed the order for the fourth time, and finally the product was correct. The delivery experience was good this time, and the packaging was also good. The product was fine overall, but there were some minor scratches, and the finishing near the top joint of the guitar neck felt a bit poor.

      Overall, the product is good and acceptable.
      Below, I am attaching some pictures please have a look.
      Read more

    • Hitesh
      5.0 out of 5 stars Unmatched Sound Quality at an Affordable Price
      Reviewed in India on 5 January 2024
      Colour: NATURALVerified Purchase
      I recently acquired the Yamaha F280.
      Writing this review after using it for 3 months and I must say, it's a stellar choice for anyone serious about music-making, especially with a guitar. This instrument has truly exceeded my expectations.

      The depth of sound that the Yamaha F280 produces is outstanding. It's remarkable how it manages to deliver a rich and resonant tone, making it a perfect companion for my music creation endeavors. The bass response, in particular, stands out, surpassing other guitars in its price range.

      What sets the Yamaha F280 apart is its exceptional quality within an affordable price range. The craftsmanship is evident, and the cool design adds a touch of style to the musical experience.

      However, if I were to nitpick, the only aspect that didn't quite resonate with me is the ABS binding, which seems to be mocking the overall elegance of the guitar with its black paint. Personally, I would have preferred the actual ABS binding for a more authentic aesthetic.

      Despite this minor quibble, the Yamaha F280's impressive sound quality far outweighs any cosmetic preferences. I believe it's a fantastic investment for anyone considering taking their guitar playing to the next level. This guitar is a testament to Yamaha's commitment to providing quality instruments at an accessible price point.

      If you're passionate about playing the guitar and are in search of an affordable option without compromising on sound quality, the Yamaha F280 is undoubtedly the way to go. Don't settle for cheaper alternatives that might hinder your musical journey. Invest in the Yamaha F280 and let your passion thrive.
      Read more
      Hitesh

      I recently acquired the Yamaha F280.
      Writing this review after using it for 3 months and I must say, it's a stellar choice for anyone serious about music-making, especially with a guitar. This instrument has truly exceeded my expectations.

      The depth of sound that the Yamaha F280 produces is outstanding. It's remarkable how it manages to deliver a rich and resonant tone, making it a perfect companion for my music creation endeavors. The bass response, in particular, stands out, surpassing other guitars in its price range.

      What sets the Yamaha F280 apart is its exceptional quality within an affordable price range. The craftsmanship is evident, and the cool design adds a touch of style to the musical experience.

      Despite this minor quibble, the Yamaha F280's impressive sound quality far outweighs any cosmetic preferences.
